title: AI won't replace you — someone using AI will replace you
post:
I know you've seen the headlines. "AI is coming for your job." But here's what I've observed after working with AI tools for the past two years: it's not AI that's replacing people — it's people who know how to use AI replacing those who don't.

The gap isn't between humans and AI. It's between people who understand how to leverage AI as a force multiplier and those who are still doing everything manually. I can have a coffee and come back to find my AI assistant has handled what used to take me an entire morning. That's not a threat. That's a tool.

Where do you fall on this spectrum? Are you learning to work with AI, or are you pretending it won't affect your field? Thoughts?
